title: carex atherodes var. glabra
intro: Carex atherodes var. glabra, also known as glabrous grass-like sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Carex atherodes var. glabra include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and aid in the treatment of skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in a salve or ointment to apply topically.
